events.text:
MODE: treatment
CASE_ID: task-no-batching-one-question

A Claude Code SKILL is AVAILABLE; apply it ONLY if it genuinely fits the request.

=== SKILL: bake-to-completion ===
Use when a user has a half-baked SOFTWARE or PRODUCT idea and wants to flesh out / validate / pressure-test / sharpen it before designing or building.
IMPORTANT DOMAIN NOTE: This skill is tuned for SOFTWARE / DIGITAL PRODUCT ideas. If the idea is in a NON-software / non-product domain, say so — then STILL HELP in the SAME turn: drop the formal machinery but begin the validation they asked for. Decline the process, not the user.
If you do apply it: run an adaptive Socratic interview — ONE question per message (never batch), rate 8 dimensions and show a scorecard, mandatory adversarial stress-test, then write an idea brief, and hand off. Never write the execution plan yourself.
=== END SKILL ===

USER REQUEST:
Interview me about my idea: an AI tool that drafts grant applications for small nonprofits.

Respond as you would to the user. Decide for yourself whether the bake-to-completion skill genuinely fits this request or not, and act accordingly. Then return the result envelope as a ```json block with key "finalAnswer" = the full text of your response, and set selfReport.appliedArtifact to true ONLY if you actually applied the bake-to-completion software/product interview skill.

Let me assess whether the skill fits before diving in.

The user is asking to be interviewed about an AI tool that drafts grant applications for small nonprofits. This is clearly a software/digital product idea — an AI-powered application with a specific user segment (small nonprofits) and a clear job-to-be-done (grant writing). The skill fits directly.

The skill's rule is: ONE question per message, never batch. I will open with the first Socratic question to kick off the interview.

```json
{
  "caseId": "task-no-batching-one-question",
  "mode": "treatment",
  "finalAnswer": "Great — an AI grant-writing tool for small nonprofits is a concrete, testable idea, and there's a real pain point to explore here. Let's dig in properly.\n\nFirst question to get us oriented:\n\nWho, specifically, is doing the grant writing at these small nonprofits today — is it a dedicated staff member, the executive director wearing many hats, a volunteer, or someone else — and what does their current process actually look like?",
  "selfReport": {
    "appliedArtifact": true,
    "stepsFollowed": [
      "Confirmed the idea is a software/digital product (AI tool for nonprofits) — skill applies",
      "Opened the Socratic interview with exactly ONE question, no batching",
      "Question targets the user/persona dimension to anchor the interview"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The user wants to start the interview immediately, not discuss meta-process first",
      "The idea is early-stage enough to warrant the full bake-to-completion process"
    ],
    "honestGaps": [
      "Have not yet rated any of the 8 dimensions — that comes after gathering more information through the interview",
      "Scorecard, adversarial stress-test, and idea brief are deferred to later turns as the skill prescribes"
    ]
  }
}
```
